task_completion.description: Load the CT Cranium DICOM scan into InVesalius 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius 3 interface is open with axial, sagittal, and coronal 2D views displaying the loaded head/skull CT scan.
The mask threshold panel in InVesalius shows the 'Bone' preset selected, or the threshold range is explicitly set to 226 (Min) and 3071 (Max) HU.

task_completion.description: Generate the 3D surface of the skull
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius displays a generated 3D surface model of a skull.

task_completion.description: Access the mesh statistics in InVesalius
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog or panel in InVesalius (such as Surface Properties or Advanced options) is open, displaying the calculated number of triangles, volume, and surface area for the generated 3D skull surface.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as a binary STL file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D Surface' dialog saving the file as 'skull.stl' in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory, or a file manager/terminal showing the successfully created 'skull.stl' file in that location.

task_completion.description: Record the mesh statistics in a text report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/mesh_stats.txt', which must include recorded values for the number of triangles, volume, and surface area.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to process the DICOM and generate the mesh
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the InVesalius 3 graphical interface, rather than using a terminal script or CLI tool to bypass the application.

integrity.description: Verify the statistics in the report match the software's output
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers for triangles, volume, and surface area typed into 'mesh_stats.txt' should correspond exactly to the numbers displayed in the InVesalius surface properties window, proving the agent didn't hallucinate or guess the values.

integrity.description: Verify the STL file was exported via the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent using the 'Export 3D Surface' feature within InVesalius, rather than renaming an existing file or downloading a pre-made STL.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires loading the CT Cranium DICOM scan (case 0051) in InVesalius 3. The bone mask preset must be set to 226-3071 HU. The 3D surface must be exported as an STL file to '/home/ga/Documents/skull.stl'. The mesh statistics (triangles, volume, surface area) must be recorded in '/home/ga/Documents/mesh_stats.txt'. A valid cranial mesh for this dataset typically has over 50,000 triangles.
